title: The White Tower's Audit
intro: Inspectors of the White Wizard Tower arrive unasked, with instruments and a cool courtesy, to audit Runehold's wards against the Fracture. Order is their gift and their judgement; how you receive them will set the Tower's word on you for years.

advisors.text: Let them look, Archon. Our wards are sound, and the White Tower's blessing is worth more than a season's pride. Show them everything.
Or show them the gate. Runehold answers to the Flame, not to the Tower's clipboards. Let them audit their own walls.

choices.label: Welcome the inspectors and open the wards to them

win.text: You open every ward to their instruments. They find them sound, tighten two, and leave a Tower's approval and a steadier hold behind.
win.saga: You welcomed the White Tower's audit; they judged Runehold's wards sound, tightening them and warming the Tower to you.

choices.label: Show them wards worthy of the Tower itself

win.text: Your order-craft startles even the Tower's own. They leave calling Runehold a model hold, and mean it.
win.saga: Your order-craft so impressed the White Tower's inspectors that they named Runehold a model hold.

lose.text: Your wards are honest but plain, and the inspectors note every fault in their cool hand. Sound enough — hardly a model.
lose.saga: The White Tower's inspectors found Runehold's wards plain but sound, warming to you only a little.

choices.label: Rebuff the audit — Runehold answers to itself

win.text: You send the inspectors back to their Tower unsatisfied. Runehold keeps its pride and its privacy — and loses a powerful friend it never quite had.
win.saga: You rebuffed the White Tower's audit; Runehold kept its independence and lost the Tower's regard for it.
